page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 20 vCPUs mapped to 20 physical cores with a single thread per core. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding 3.2 GB of memory per core. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, reflecting the shared CPU allocation and modest 2.0 GHz clock speed. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, achieve average performance tiers. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and prompt processing for a small 135M parameter LLM model register strong performance in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and standard database operations remain within average parameters.

This server offers a high core density at a qualitatively cost-efficient profile, making it suitable for parallelizable workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated single-core performance. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which can introduce performance variability under peak loads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations where resource sharing is acceptable.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 20 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on an x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data reveals a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, landing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and small LLM prompt processing, achieve average to strong results. This resource profile offers high core density for parallel tasks but introduces potential variability due to its shared CPU allocation. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and software compilation where sustained single-core performance is not critical.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server with 20 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, offering average multi-core performance but limited single-core capabilities.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a KVM server with 20 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, suited for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core-density virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 20 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
64.0 GB of system memory with no local storage bundled
Strong multi-threaded decompression and small LLM prompt processing
Poor single-core performance due to shared CPU allocation
Well-suited for parallelized tasks, software compilation, and web serving
